content.text:
All 3 tests pass with the existing implementation `return []`. The new test "should kill two adjacent live cells due to underpopulation" passes coincidentally because both adjacent live cells die from underpopulation (each has only 1 neighbor), so the next generation is correctly empty.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: No code change needed - existing `return []` correctly handles this case
- Result: All 3 tests pass (4 todo remaining)
- Approach: This is the minimal possible implementation. The test passes coincidentally because the expected output for `[[0,1],[1,1]]` is indeed `[]` (both live cells die from underpopulation with only 1 neighbor each). Adding logic now would be over-implementation - we should let future tests force generalization.

Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_11-31-08_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/game-of-life.ts
